compiled_state.title: Replace SIGPIPE-vulnerable echo|grep -q patterns in test suite
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Developers and CI systems experience intermittent test failures (exit 141 / SIGPIPE) when echo "$var" | grep -q "pattern" runs under set -o pipefail. The shared run_test.sh was already fixed, but 602 occurrences across 126 individual test files remain. This affects every commit that triggers the test suite.

## Success Criteria
- Zero echo ... | grep -q patterns remain in tests/ (verified by grep -rn 'echo.*|.*grep -q' tests/ | wc -l returning 0)
- All replaced patterns preserve original matching semantics (case-insensitive -i, extended regex -E, fixed-string -F, negated !)
- Full test suite passes after replacement (bash tests/run-all.sh)
- No new SIGPIPE (exit 141) failures in CI after merge

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Batch sub-agent replacement: 5 parallel agents per batch, ~25 files each. Flag-to-bash mapping: grep -q -> [[ =~ ]], grep -qi -> shopt -s nocasematch + [[ =~ ]], grep -qF -> [[ == *pattern* ]], grep -qE -> [[ =~ ]] (bash regex is ERE by default), negated -> ! [[ =~ ]].

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): not triggered
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none

comments.body: Epic complete: all tasks closed, completion-verifier PASS on all 4 success criteria, branch merged to main (78ece073). Zero pipe|grep -q patterns remain across entire test suite (602 echo-based + 37 non-echo = 639 total replacements across 154 files).
